event: diagnosed to have tropical chronic pancreatitis
presented to a medical center in India
pancreatography confirmed dilated duct and intraductal calculi
planned removal of ductal stones once acute exacerbation resolved
presented to Saint Peter’s University Hospital
hospitalized for pain control with narcotics
returned complaining of continuing epigastric pain
pancreatic duct filled with large calcifications
possible obstruction at the level of the neck
marked upstream ductal dilatation of 1.5 cm
admitted for management of pain secondary to chronic pancreatitis
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
no obvious obstructing calculi (common bile duct)
major papilla of Vater spontaneously expelling frank pus
evacuation of more than 5 ml of yellow pus
fluid could not be aspirated for culture
marked dilatation of the main pancreatic duct
guide wire introduced into the pancreatic duct
approximately 20 ml of pus evacuated during duct manipulation
5-cm-long 5 F stent placed into the pancreatic duct
no more pus draining after stent placement
acute suppuration of the pancreatic duct (ASPD)
contrast enhanced CT scan of abdomen
inflammatory changes within peripancreatic fat consistent with edema
dilatation of the pancreatic duct diminished
calculus distal migration towards the sphincter of Oddi
Klebsiella ornithinolytica sensitive to the antibiotics given
transferred from the intensive care unit
